title: Analyse & record ≥5 candidate standards; approve the open core
context: Before any vocabulary binding or crosswalk work can proceed, the licence reviewer must analyse and approve candidate terminology and data standards. The open-lead rule (Mondo/ORDO/HPO/OncoTree/NCIt/mCODE/OMOP/Phenopackets/DUO) and the restricted-stub rule (SNOMED CT/ICD-O-3/LOINC/ICCR) defined in license-001 must be populated with concrete licence determinations for at least 5 candidate standards. OMOP Standardized Vocabularies carry mixed per-vocab licences requiring special analysis. No sources are currently recorded in allowlist.yml. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-license-001.
objective: Research and record the licence determination for ≥5 candidate standards in `sources/allowlist.yml`, approving the open core (Mondo, ORDO, HPO, OncoTree, NCIt, mCODE, OMOP CDM, Phenopackets, DUO), and recording SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, and ICCR as restricted (stub-only). Capture the OMOP Standardized Vocabulary per-vocab licence caveat (some require SNOMED/CPT licences — open member vocabs only).
acceptanceCriteria: ≥5 candidate standards analysed with licence determination recorded in `sources/allowlist.yml` using the schema from license-001.

The open core (Mondo CC-BY-4.0, ORDO CC-BY-4.0, HPO permissive, OncoTree open/MSK, NCIt public/NCI, mCODE HL7, OMOP CDM CC-BY-4.0, GA4GH Phenopackets Apache-2.0, DUO CC-BY-4.0) recorded with status: approved.
SNOMED CT, ICD-O-3, LOINC, and ICCR recorded with restricted: true and status: rejected for embedding; stub-only use documented.
ISO/IEC 11179 recorded with a note that only the metadata-discipline model is adopted, not the copyrighted standard text.
OMOP Standardized Vocabularies caveat captured: only open member vocabs permitted; vocabs requiring SNOMED or CPT licences excluded.
Licence reviewer sign-off recorded in the allowlist or a companion reviewers ledger entry before the task is marked done.
output: Populated `sources/allowlist.yml` with ≥5 analysed standards and their licence determinations; a companion research note documenting the analysis rationale, committed via PR with licence reviewer sign-off.
